Common_Genes: VvStSy (Vitis vinifera stilbene synthase; introduced structural gene for resveratrol production in tomato fruit)
LjIFS (Lotus japonicus isoflavone synthase; introduced structural gene for genistein/genistin production in tomato fruit)
Pathway_Genes: ENO (plastidial enolase; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter bound by AtMYB12 and SlMYB12, upregulated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit, links glycolysis to shikimate pathway)

DAHPS (3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate synthase; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter bound by AtMYB12 and SlMYB12, upregulated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit, key entry enzyme of the shikimate pathway)
PAL5A (phenylalanine ammonia lyase 5A; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter significantly enriched in ChIP-qPCR)
PAL5C (phenylalanine ammonia lyase 5C; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter significantly enriched in ChIP-qPCR)
PAL5D (phenylalanine ammonia lyase 5D; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter significantly enriched in ChIP-qPCR)
CHS1 (chalcone synthase 1; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter bound confirmed by ChIP-qPCR and ChIP-seq)
F3H (flavanone 3-hydroxylase; direct target of AtMYB12, promoter significantly enriched in ChIP-qPCR; are mutant is deficient in F3H activity, used to redirect flux to isoflavones)
Regulation_Genes: AtMYB12 (Arabidopsis MYB transcription factor; directly binds promoters of ENO, DAHPS, PAL5A/C/D, CHS1, F3H via TACCTACC motif; reprograms primary and secondary metabolism increasing flavonols, hydroxycinnamates, and aromatic amino acids in tomato fruit)
SlMYB12 (Solanum lycopersicum MYB12; functional homologue of AtMYB12, expressed in tomato peel, binds same promoter motifs of ENO, DAHPS and CHS1 as AtMYB12)
Del (Delila; Antirrhinum bHLH transcription factor; activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in tomato fruit, does not upregulate primary metabolism)
Ros1 (Rosea1; Antirrhinum MYB transcription factor; activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in tomato fruit together with Del, does not upregulate primary metabolism)
Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato
